_doc: Circulation scoring rules for the WP2 reward system. Per-room-type maximum graph-step distance from the entrance room. A room scores 100 if its BFS distance <= max; 0 otherwise. Increase a value to allow deeper nesting for that room type. The entrance room itself always has distance 0.

entry_detection._doc: How to identify the entry room. 'entrance_point_on_wall' = find the room whose polygon boundary contains apartment.entrance within tolerance. If no Hallway is present, BFS starts from whichever room contains the entrance point.

max_distance_from_entrance._doc: Keys are room_type strings from ROOM_TYPES. Values contain 'max': integer max BFS steps from the entrance room. Rooms not listed here use max_distance_default.

Hallway._doc: Hallway must BE the entry room (distance 0). If found at distance 1+, the layout is inverted — scored 0 for that room.

Living room._doc: Directly off hallway or entry room.

Kitchen._doc: Directly off hallway or entry room.

Bathroom._doc: Directly off hallway or entry room.

WC._doc: Directly off hallway or entry room.

Bedroom._doc: Directly off hallway or entry room.

Children 1._doc: Can be accessed via a bedroom or directly off hallway.

Children 2._doc: Can be accessed via a bedroom or directly off hallway.

Children 3._doc: Can be accessed via a bedroom or directly off hallway.

Children 4._doc: Can be accessed via a bedroom or directly off hallway.

max_distance_default._doc: Fallback max distance for any room_type not listed in max_distance_from_entrance.

adjacency._doc: Two rooms are adjacent if any apartment-level door point (from apartment.doors) lies on the polygon boundary of both rooms within tolerance_m. This forms the undirected graph used for BFS. Door points are apartment-level — not per-room — so a Hallway with N neighbours has N door points, all resolved automatically.

aggregate._doc: How to aggregate per-room pass/fail into a single circulation score. 'mean_pass_rate' = (rooms that pass / total rooms) * 100.
